items.title: Regression vs Classification
items.description: Supervised learning splits by target type: continuous y (regression) or discrete y (classification). Output heads, metrics, and loss choices differ — detailed in the regression vs classification topic.

items.title: Unsupervised learning
items.description: Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled x only (clustering, dimensionality reduction). Supervised learning requires known y for each training example — the defining difference between the two paradigms.

items.title: Machine learning
items.description: Machine learning is the broader field of learning from data. Supervised learning is the most widely deployed ML paradigm — prediction tasks with labeled training data.

items.title: Train / validation / test splits
items.description: Supervised models are trained on labeled training sets and evaluated on held-out labeled validation/test sets to estimate generalization. Proper splits prevent optimistic metrics from training-set memorization.

items.title: Loss functions
items.description: Supervised training minimizes a loss L(ŷ, y) — MSE for regression, cross-entropy for classification. The loss converts labels into a differentiable training signal.

items.title: Training and inference
items.description: Supervised workflows separate a training phase (update θ using labeled data) from inference (predict ŷ for new x without labels). Deployment assumes the same feature distribution as training.
